title: Implement channel actor with subscriber management and message delivery
purpose: Implement the channel actor — the beamr process that owns a channel's subscriber list, delivers published messages to all matching subscribers, and handles graceful shutdown. This is the runtime heart of the channel primitive. Ephemeral channels only — no persistence (C9).
task: Build the channel actor as a beamr process. The actor owns its subscriber list, accepts publish/subscribe/unsubscribe/close commands via its mailbox, and fans out published messages to all active subscribers. Subscribers are added via process linking — when a subscriber process dies, the link notification automatically removes it from the list (C11). The actor is supervised (C8) so a crash restarts the individual channel without affecting others. Predicate-filtered subscriptions (C12) evaluate the predicate at delivery time and skip non-matching subscribers. Ephemeral channels have zero persistence overhead (C9). Also implement the channel registry — a process that maps channel names to actor PIDs and provides create/lookup/list/close operations.

requirements.title: Implement channel actor process
requirements.spec: The channel actor SHALL be a beamr process that receives commands (Publish, Subscribe, Unsubscribe, Close, ListSubscribers) via its mailbox. The actor SHALL own its subscriber list as an in-memory data structure. WHEN the actor receives a Publish command, it SHALL deliver the message to all active subscribers. WHEN the actor receives a Close command, it SHALL notify all subscribers and terminate normally.
acceptance: Channel actor is spawned as a beamr process via Scheduler::spawn
Actor handles Publish, Subscribe, Unsubscribe, Close, and ListSubscribers commands
Publish fans out the message to all active subscribers
Close sends a channel-closed notification to all subscribers before terminating
Actor mailbox processing uses selective receive

requirements.title: Implement subscriber management with process linking
requirements.spec: WHEN a subscriber process subscribes to a channel, the channel actor SHALL link to the subscriber process. IF the subscriber process dies, THEN the channel actor SHALL automatically remove it from the subscriber list via the EXIT signal. Unsubscribe SHALL unlink and remove the subscriber.
acceptance: Subscribe links the channel actor to the subscriber PID via Scheduler::spawn_link or equivalent
Subscriber process death triggers EXIT signal in the channel actor
EXIT signal handler removes the dead subscriber from the list
Unsubscribe unlinks and removes the subscriber
Test: subscriber process dies → channel actor continues delivering to remaining subscribers

requirements.title: Implement predicate-filtered subscriptions
requirements.spec: WHEN a subscriber subscribes with a predicate filter, the channel actor SHALL evaluate the predicate against each published message. The actor SHALL only deliver messages where the predicate returns true. IF no predicate is provided, the subscriber SHALL receive all messages.
acceptance: SubscriptionConfig accepts an optional predicate (a closure or function reference)
Channel actor evaluates the predicate per-message per-subscriber at delivery time
Messages not matching the predicate are not delivered to that subscriber
Subscribers without a predicate receive all messages
Test: two subscribers — one filtered, one unfiltered — different delivery sets

requirements.title: Implement channel supervision
requirements.spec: Channel actors SHALL be supervised. IF a channel actor crashes, THEN the supervisor SHALL restart it without affecting other channel actors. The supervision tree SHALL be configurable (restart strategy, max restarts).
acceptance: Channel actors are spawned under a supervisor process
A crashed channel actor is restarted by the supervisor
Other channel actors are unaffected by the crash
Test: crash one channel actor → verify other channels continue delivering

requirements.title: Enforce zero persistence overhead for ephemeral channels
requirements.spec: Ephemeral channels SHALL NOT invoke any persistence, serialisation, or disk I/O operations. Message delivery SHALL be pure in-memory message passing through beamr mailboxes. WHILE a channel is in Ephemeral mode, it SHALL NOT reference or import haematite types.
acceptance: Ephemeral channel actor code path has no haematite imports or calls
No serialisation of messages in the ephemeral delivery path
No disk I/O or async runtime interaction in the ephemeral delivery path
Test: publish-subscribe round trip on ephemeral channel completes without any persistence artifact

requirements.title: Implement channel registry
requirements.spec: A channel registry SHALL map channel names to actor PIDs. The registry SHALL support create (spawn a new channel actor), lookup (find by name), list (all active channels with subscriber counts), and close (signal a channel to shut down). The registry SHALL reject duplicate channel names.
acceptance: Registry provides create(config) -> Result<ChannelHandle, LiminalError>
Registry provides lookup(name) -> Option<ChannelHandle>
Registry provides list() -> Vec<ChannelInfo> with name and subscriber count
Registry provides close(name) -> Result<(), LiminalError>
Duplicate channel name returns LiminalError::ChannelAlreadyExists or equivalent
Test: create → lookup → list → close lifecycle

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement schema validation — that is LIM-003.
SHALL NOT implement the conversation actor — that is LIM-004.
SHALL NOT add any persistence or haematite integration.
SHALL NOT implement routing logic — that is the routing cluster.
SHALL NOT implement the wire protocol — that is the protocol cluster.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
Channel actor is a beamr process, not a Rust struct with methods.
Subscriber death automatically removes from the subscriber list.
Ephemeral channel delivery path has no persistence calls.
